# ImDy Per-Person Physics Plausibility — Experiment Report

**Date:** 2026-04-01  
**Experiment ID:** imdy_perperson_2026-04-01  
**Author:** Auto-generated by eval pipeline  

## Objective

Evaluate the physical plausibility of generated two-person interaction motions using ImDy (ICLR 2025), a learned inverse dynamics model. Compare GT motions against generations from **InterMask** and **InterGen** on per-person torque and GRF metrics.

## Setup

### Model
- **ImDy checkpoint:** `prepare5/ImDy/downloaded_checkpoint/imdy_pretrain.pt` (epoch 149)
- **Config:** `prepare5/ImDy/config/IDFD_mkr.yml`
- **Architecture:** mkrIDFD — transformer decoder (3 layers, dim=64, 4 heads)
- **Input:** 22 joint positions as markers (within ImDy's training range of 22–96 random markers)

### Preprocessing
- **Window:** 6 frames (past_kf=2, fut_kf=2)
- **Treadmill:** XY-centered at pelvis of center frame
- **Heading removal:** disabled
- **FPS:** 30 Hz
- **Velocity:** central-difference on windowed data

### Scoring
- **Mode:** per-person only (no interaction metrics in this run)
- **Contact masking:** disabled (raw GRF used)
- **Body weight:** 75 kg (default)

### Datasets

| Dataset | Source | Layout | Clips | Scored | Failed |
|---------|--------|--------|------:|-------:|-------:|
| GT InterHuman | `data/retargeted_v2/interhuman` | retargeted npy | 7810 | 7806 | 4 |
| GT InterX | `data/retargeted_v2/interx` | retargeted npy | 11388 | 11387 | 1 |
| InterMask Gen (InterHuman) | `data/retargeted_v2/generated_interhuman` | retargeted npy | 1098 | 1098 | 0 |
| InterMask Gen (InterX) | `data/retargeted_v2/generated_interx` | retargeted npy | 1706 | 1706 | 0 |
| InterGen Gen (InterHuman) | `data/generated_intergen/interhuman` | positions_pkl | 1177 | 1173 | 4 |

Failures are due to clips shorter than 6 frames (window_len).

## Results

### Per-Person Metrics — GT vs Generated (Person 1)

#### InterHuman Test Set (GT=7806 clips)

| Metric | GT | InterMask (1098) | InterGen (1173) |
|--------|---:|---:|---:|
| Mean Torque (BW-norm) | 0.2592 | 0.2479 (Δ=-0.011) | 0.2511 (Δ=-0.008) |
| Torque 95th pctl (Nm) | 386.72 | 362.73 (Δ=-24.0) | 365.97 (Δ=-20.8) |
| Torque Smoothness (Nm/frame) | 85.83 | 108.19 (Δ=+22.4) | 73.81 (Δ=-12.0) |
| Negative GRF Rate | 0.2120 | 0.1923 (Δ=-0.020) | 0.2063 (Δ=-0.006) |
| Mean Vert GRF (N) | 13.14 | 12.04 (Δ=-1.10) | 12.24 (Δ=-0.90) |
| Contact Rate | 0.1616 | 0.1645 (Δ=+0.003) | 0.1624 (Δ=+0.001) |
| Contact-Height Corr | 0.1846 | 0.0461 (Δ=-0.139) | 0.1490 (Δ=-0.036) |

#### InterX Test Set (GT=11387 clips)

| Metric | GT | InterMask (1706) |
|--------|---:|---:|
| Mean Torque (BW-norm) | 0.2622 | 0.2588 (Δ=-0.003) |
| Torque 95th pctl (Nm) | 388.32 | 380.77 (Δ=-7.55) |
| Torque Smoothness (Nm/frame) | 51.51 | 35.23 (Δ=-16.3) |
| Negative GRF Rate | 0.1448 | 0.1436 (Δ=-0.001) |
| Mean Vert GRF (N) | 8.65 | 7.37 (Δ=-1.29) |
| Contact Rate | 0.1685 | 0.1625 (Δ=-0.006) |
| Contact-Height Corr | 0.0217 | -0.0114 (Δ=-0.033) |

### Statistical Significance

All differences except Torque Violation Rate are statistically significant (KS test p < 0.001). Torque Violation Rate is 0% everywhere (not discriminative — all torques within biomechanical limits).

### Wasserstein Distances (distribution divergence, lower = closer to GT)

| Metric | InterMask (IH) | InterGen (IH) | InterMask (IX) |
|--------|---:|---:|---:|
| Mean Torque (BW) | 0.0115 | 0.0083 | 0.0043 |
| Torque 95th (Nm) | 24.92 | 20.76 | 8.18 |
| Torque Smoothness | 24.21 | 12.53 | 16.28 |
| Neg GRF Rate | 0.0262 | 0.0081 | 0.0030 |
| Contact-Height Corr | 0.1459 | 0.0467 | 0.0424 |

## Key Findings

1. **InterGen produces more physically plausible motions than InterMask** on InterHuman:
   - Torque smoothness: InterGen is 14% smoother than GT; InterMask is 26% jerkier than GT
   - Contact-height consistency: InterGen preserves 81% of GT correlation (0.149 vs 0.185); InterMask only 25% (0.046)
   - Wasserstein distances are 2–3× smaller for InterGen across all metrics

2. **Both methods produce lower peak torques than GT**, suggesting generated motions are less dynamic / more conservative in movement intensity.

3. **Torque Violation Rate is not discriminative** — all methods stay within biomechanical torque limits. The clamping in ImDy's postprocessing (clampLayer) may contribute to this.

4. **Negative GRF Rate (~14–21%)** indicates some non-physical "pulling" ground forces across all datasets. This may reflect ImDy's prediction noise rather than actual physics violations.

5. **InterX GT has lower torque smoothness values than InterHuman GT** (51.5 vs 85.8), suggesting InterX motions have slower/smoother movements on average.

## Limitations

- ImDy predicts torques for a **generic body** (no per-subject mass/inertia), so absolute torque values are approximate.
- The model was trained on single-person data; predictions don't account for interaction forces.
- Per-person metrics only — interaction-level analysis (paired-vs-solo, N3L) deferred to next experiment.
- Torque violation rate is uninformative due to ImDy's built-in clamping.

## Reproduction

```bash
# Score a dataset
/data2/rh/conda_envs/torchv2/bin/python eval_pipeline/imdy_scorer.py \
    --data-dir data/retargeted_v2/generated_interhuman \
    --dataset-type retargeted \
    --output-dir data/imdy_metrics/generated_interhuman \
    --per-person-only \
    --device cuda:0

# Score InterGen (PKL format)
/data2/rh/conda_envs/torchv2/bin/python eval_pipeline/imdy_scorer.py \
    --data-dir data/generated_intergen/interhuman \
    --dataset-type positions_pkl \
    --output-dir data/imdy_metrics/generated_intergen_interhuman \
    --per-person-only \
    --device cuda:0

# Compare GT vs Generated
/data2/rh/conda_envs/torchv2/bin/python eval_pipeline/imdy_scorer.py --compare \
    --gt-dir data/imdy_metrics/gt_interhuman \
    --gen-dir data/imdy_metrics/generated_interhuman \
    --output data/imdy_metrics/comparison_interhuman.json

# Full batch (all datasets, both GPUs)
nohup bash scripts/run_imdy_perperson.sh > /tmp/imdy_perperson_all.log 2>&1 &

# Visualize per-clip predictions
/data2/rh/conda_envs/torchv2/bin/python eval_pipeline/visualize_imdy.py \
    --data-dir data/retargeted_v2/generated_interhuman \
    --output-dir output/imdy_viz/generated_interhuman \
    --clip-ids 1004,1006 \
    --device cuda:0
```

## Output Files

| Path | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `data/imdy_metrics/*/summary.json` | Per-dataset aggregate statistics |
| `data/imdy_metrics/*/{clip_id}_imdy.json` | Per-clip metrics |
| `data/imdy_metrics/comparison_interhuman.json` | InterMask (IH) vs GT comparison |
| `data/imdy_metrics/comparison_intergen_interhuman.json` | InterGen (IH) vs GT comparison |
| `data/imdy_metrics/comparison_interx.json` | InterMask (IX) vs GT comparison |
| `output/imdy_viz/` | Per-clip torque/GRF visualizations |
